title: Centrapalus africanus
intro: Centrapalus africanus, also known as African Centaury or African Cent, is a type of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to southern Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Centrapalus africanus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and also used in the form of capsules and tablets, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments.
taxonomy: Centrapalus africanus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, which includes daisies and sunflowers. This plant is a member of the tribe Inuleae. It is also known by its synonym, Inula crithmifolia, which further highlights its classification within the Asteraceae family. The genus Centrapalus is a group of plants that are native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Africa. As part of the Asteraceae family, Centrapalus africanus shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as composite flowers and a similar growth habit.
